name: ION-14382 Booking DynamoDB AWS SDK v2 Upgrade Review

entries.summary: CRITICAL: Backward compatibility issue with DynamoDB data format. Old code used @DynamoDBDocument (Map type) for Audit, EnrichedAttributes, MetaData, Condition, Range. New converters expect JSON String (S type). Existing DynamoDB records will lose data when read.
entries.detail: Affected converters: AuditAttributeConverter, EnrichedAttributesConverter, MetaDataConverter, ConditionListAttributeConverter, RangeAttributeConverter. Affected tables: BookingDetail (549 items), SpotRatesToInttraRefDetail (11 items), RapidReservation (9 items). Need to add dual-format support in all converters.

entries.summary: GSI Projection Type mismatch: Code uses ProjectionType.ALL but production uses KEYS_ONLY for all BookingDetail, SpotRatesDetail, SpotRatesToInttraRefDetail GSIs
entries.detail: BookingDetail model has all 4 GSIs annotated with @GsiConfig(projection = ProjectionType.ALL) but the actual DynamoDB table uses KEYS_ONLY projection for all GSIs. This is a minor discrepancy that only affects table creation (in tests/local setup). Does not affect runtime queries since DAOs re-fetch from main table.

entries.summary: All unit tests (1683) and integration tests (149) PASS after converter backward compatibility fixes and GSI projection type corrections
entries.detail: Unit tests: 1683 run, 0 failures (1 skipped - pre-existing). Integration tests: 149 run, 0 failures, 0 errors. All DynamoDB-related integration tests pass including BookingDetailDaoIT, SpotRatesDaoIT, SpotRatesToInttraRefDaoIT, RapidReservationDaoIT, TemplateDaoIT, TemplateSummaryDaoIT, SequenceIdDaoIT, UniqueIdDaoIT.

entries.summary: Documentation complete. All tasks finished: aws-dynamo-review.md created with table-by-table analysis, annotation migration tables, converter details, backward compatibility explanation, GSI fixes, DAO migration, test results, and risk assessment.
entries.detail: Created booking/docs/aws-dynamo-review.md with: (1) Executive summary with findings table, (2) 7-table overview, (3) Detailed model-by-model comparison with before/after annotation tables, (4) GSI definitions with ASCII diagrams, (5) Converter migration table with dual-format explanation, (6) DAO migration details with repository types, (7) Guice module structure, (8) Changes made during review, (9) Test results (1683 unit + 149 integration), (10) Risk assessment table. Model used: Claude Opus 4.6
